questions.scenario: Only VMs running as `orders-worker` should call an internal VM backend. Which target/source habit is usually stronger than allowing every instance in the VPC?

options.text: Use workload-specific targeting such as service-account or narrow tag-based rules where appropriate.
Use the billing account as a firewall target.
Use a project description as the source.

questions.explanation: Targets should match the workload role as narrowly as the design supports.

questions.scenario: A team tries to attach a normal VM network tag firewall target directly to a Cloud Run instance. What is the better mental model?

options.text: Cloud Run uses service-level ingress and configured egress paths; it is not a tagged VM instance.
Cloud Run cannot receive HTTPS requests.
Cloud Run firewall rules are billing labels.

questions.explanation: Cloud Run networking is controlled through service settings and egress configuration rather than VM instance tags.

questions.scenario: Which statements about firewall targets are healthy?

options.text: All-instance targets are broad and should be reviewed carefully.
Network tags can drift if copied casually.
Service-account targeting can express workload role for VMs.
A target is unnecessary because every allow rule should apply everywhere.

questions.explanation: The target keeps a useful allow from becoming a network-wide allow.
